profile.name: Richard Doty
roles: Special Agent, U.S. Air Force Office of Special Investigations (AFOSI), Kirtland AFB
Primary operative in the Paul Bennewitz disinformation operation (1980-1988)
organizations: Air Force Office of Special Investigations (AFOSI)
profile.summary: Retired U.S. Air Force Office of Special Investigations (AFOSI) special agent and the most consequential documented government disinformation operative in UAP research history. Doty was the primary AFOSI agent responsible for a sustained psychological operation against defense contractor Paul Bennewitz (1980-1988) that fed fabricated UAP evidence to Bennewitz - and, through him, to the broader research community - ultimately contributing to Bennewitz's mental breakdown. Doty also showed journalist Linda Moulton Howe a fabricated classified presidential briefing document on extraterrestrial intelligence at Kirtland AFB in 1983, and supplied doctored documents to researcher Bill Moore. He has publicly acknowledged these activities post-retirement. His significance is not as a reliable source but as evidence that the deliberate contamination of the UAP evidentiary record by U.S. intelligence agencies is a documented historical fact.
education: U.S. Air Force enlisted training and AFOSI special agent certification
early_career: Enlisted in the U.S. Air Force and trained as an AFOSI counterintelligence special agent
Assigned to Kirtland AFB, New Mexico - a major Air Force installation adjacent to Sandia National Laboratories and Kirtland's nuclear weapons storage facilities
AFOSI's counterintelligence mandate at Kirtland included monitoring civilian UAP researchers who were in proximity to classified facilities and sensitive programs

key_events.event: Assigned as an AFOSI special agent to Kirtland AFB, New Mexico. His primary counterintelligence responsibilities included monitoring civilian researchers and journalists who were investigating UAP activity in the vicinity of Kirtland's nuclear weapons facilities - a role that placed him at the intersection of AFOSI's security mandate and the UAP research community.

The Bennewitz Affair begins. Paul Bennewitz, a defense contractor and amateur radio operator living adjacent to Kirtland AFB, began intercepting what he believed were alien transmissions from craft operating near the base - which were in fact signals from a classified program. Doty was assigned as the primary AFOSI operative to manage Bennewitz. Rather than simply dismissing him, AFOSI ran a deliberate psychological operation: Doty began feeding Bennewitz an escalating series of fabricated documents, photographs, and narratives about underground alien bases, human-ET agreements, and alien abductions, designed to overwhelm him with false information and discredit his legitimate observations of classified activity.

In April 1983, Doty invited Emmy Award-winning journalist Linda Moulton Howe to Kirtland AFB under the pretense of providing her with classified UAP material for a television documentary. He showed her what he described as a classified presidential briefing document detailing extraterrestrial contact and government agreements with alien species. She was allowed to read but not copy it. The document was fabricated. This encounter is documented in Howe's sworn affidavit and is a key event in understanding how disinformation penetrated credible journalism.

Supplied fabricated UAP documents to researcher Bill Moore, who was simultaneously acting as an AFOSI informant monitoring the UAP research community. Moore later publicly confessed to this arrangement at a MUFON symposium in 1989, acknowledging he had knowingly spread disinformation in exchange for access to what he was led to believe was genuine classified material. This established a documented chain: AFOSI used civilian researchers as both targets and vectors for disinformation.

Appeared anonymously as 'Falcon' on the nationally broadcast television special 'UFO Cover-Up? Live!,' providing fabricated insider accounts of government alien contact programs - including details about extraterrestrials' taste for strawberry ice cream and Tibetan music - broadcast to millions of viewers. Retired from AFOSI the same year. Paul Bennewitz, the primary target of the operation Doty managed, was hospitalized for mental illness around this period, a breakdown his family and associates attributed in part to the disinformation campaign.

Documentary filmmaker Mark Pilkington released 'Mirage Men,' the most comprehensive public examination of Doty's disinformation activities. Doty participated in the film, partially acknowledging his role. The documentary established the Bennewitz affair as a documented case study in deliberate government contamination of the UAP research record.

Post-retirement, Doty began appearing regularly at UAP conferences and on podcasts - including appearances on George Knapp's Weaponized platform - simultaneously claiming to acknowledge past disinformation activities while presenting himself as having genuine insider knowledge of real UAP programs. This creates a structurally unresolvable credibility problem: a documented disinformation agent's claims about real programs are epistemically indistinguishable from continued disinformation operations.

claims.claim: AFOSI conducted a deliberate psychological operation against Paul Bennewitz, feeding him fabricated UAP evidence to discredit his observations of legitimate classified activity near Kirtland AFB.
claims.notes: This is one of the few fully verified claims in UAP history. Doty has personally acknowledged the core mechanics of the operation in multiple interviews and in the 'Mirage Men' documentary. Bill Moore's 1989 MUFON confession corroborates it from a second participant. Bennewitz's documented mental deterioration and hospitalization are consistent with the described sustained psychological pressure. The FBI assessed documents associated with the operation as fabricated. No credible source disputes the basic facts of the Bennewitz affair.

claims.claim: Doty possesses genuine insider knowledge of classified UAP programs separate from his documented disinformation activities.
claims.notes: This is the structurally unresolvable problem with treating Doty as a source. He has made this claim repeatedly post-retirement. However, a documented professional disinformation agent's claims about real programs are epistemically indistinguishable from continued disinformation. Multiple credible UAP researchers - including Robert Hastings and Jacques Vallee - have explicitly warned against treating Doty's post-retirement statements as reliable. The claim cannot be verified or falsified from the outside, which may itself be by design.

supporting: The Bennewitz affair is one of the best-documented cases in UAP history precisely because Doty acknowledged it and a second participant (Moore) confirmed it - making him an unusually transparent disinformation operative
His AFOSI credentials and Kirtland AFB assignment are confirmed - he was genuinely an institutional actor with counterintelligence authority

His willingness to appear in 'Mirage Men' and acknowledge past operations, while self-serving in some respects, provides more institutional accountability than most intelligence community actors in this space
contradicting: Doty is by definition an unreliable source: he is a documented, self-acknowledged professional disinformation agent whose career involved fabricating evidence specifically for the UAP research community

The partial admissions in 'Mirage Men' and subsequent interviews serve his post-retirement persona as an 'insider' and may themselves be a continuation of controlled information management rather than genuine transparency
Multiple respected UAP researchers - Hastings, Vallee, and others - have explicitly warned against treating Doty's post-retirement statements as reliable
His claimed 'real' insider knowledge cannot be distinguished from continued disinformation by any available external method
Accusations of post-retirement surveillance of UAP researchers have been reported by KLAS-TV Las Vegas

associated_people.name: George Knapp
associated_people.relationship: Knapp has covered Doty's activities through his decades of UAP investigative journalism at KLAS-TV and has interviewed him extensively for Weaponized and other platforms. The relationship reflects the tension between Knapp's investigative approach and the epistemological problem of using a documented disinformation agent as a source.

associated_people.name: Stanton T. Friedman
associated_people.relationship: Friedman spent years analyzing the MJ-12 documents that were part of the disinformation ecosystem Doty helped create. Friedman was a target of the disinformation efforts insofar as fabricated documents contaminated the evidentiary record he was trying to authenticate. He was publicly skeptical of Doty's reliability as a source.

associated_people.name: Richard Dolan
associated_people.relationship: Dolan has documented the Bennewitz affair and Doty's disinformation operations in 'UFOs and the National Security State' and related work, providing the most rigorous historical context for understanding Doty's impact on the UAP research community.

disclosures.title: UFO Cover-Up? Live! (as 'Falcon')
disclosures.notes: Appeared anonymously as 'Falcon' on this nationally broadcast special, providing fabricated accounts of government alien contact programs. The appearance represents the most public face of the AFOSI disinformation operation during Doty's active service period. He was speaking to an audience of millions with fabricated material.

disclosures.title: Mirage Men
disclosures.notes: Participated in Mark Pilkington's documentary examining the Bennewitz affair and AFOSI disinformation operations. Partially acknowledged his role in the psychological operation against Paul Bennewitz. The documentary is the most comprehensive public record of his activities and their consequences.

career_connections.relationship: Friedman spent years attempting to authenticate MJ-12 documents that were part of the disinformation ecosystem Doty helped create; his painstaking work was directly undermined by fabricated materials introduced into the evidentiary record through the Bennewitz operation.

Knapp investigated and documented Doty's activities through decades of UAP journalism at KLAS-TV and has interviewed him on Weaponized, creating a complex relationship between an investigative journalist and a documented disinformation operative who presents as a source.

Dolan documented the Bennewitz affair and Doty's operations in 'UFOs and the National Security State,' providing the most rigorous historical analysis of how AFOSI disinformation contaminated the UAP research record.

sources.title: Mirage Men - Mark Pilkington (2013 documentary)
sources.notes: The definitive documentary examination of Doty's disinformation activities and the Bennewitz affair. Doty participated in the film. Primary source for the most complete public account of his operations and their consequences.

sources.title: Mirage Men - Mark Pilkington (2010 book)
sources.notes: The book companion to the documentary, providing more detailed historical context for the Bennewitz affair and AFOSI's broader disinformation operations targeting the UAP research community.

sources.title: UFOs and the National Security State - Richard Dolan (2002)
sources.notes: Dolan's rigorous historical documentation of the Bennewitz affair and AFOSI disinformation operations, providing essential context for understanding Doty's role and its institutional significance.

sources.title: Bill Moore MUFON Confession (1989)
sources.notes: Moore's 1989 public confession at the MUFON symposium, in which he acknowledged acting as an AFOSI informant while spreading disinformation. Corroborating second-participant account of the operation Doty managed.
